content.text:
{
  "dimension": "methodology-fidelity",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The coach uses several AoA-flavored moves competently in the first half: somatic check-in ('Where do you feel it in your body right now') at turn 4 is a signature question deployed at the right moment; 'what's it bracing against?' at turn 6 functions like the 'what are you defending against?' family of questions; the validation of the child's adaptation at turn 8 echoes the AoA stance toward protective patterns. However, at turn 13 the client explicitly signals somatic shutdown ('I'm getting cold,' 'I went somewhere,' flatter voice) — a clear dissociation signal where the methodology calls for grounding (feet, breath, present-moment orientation, calibrate-the-threshold). Instead, the coach in turn 14 returns to the trauma material with 'do you remember what you were about to feel?' This is a misapplication of the feel-the-unfelt-emotion move in an inappropriate_when context (dissociation/freeze). The 'you don't have to come back faster than you come back' line is gentle and AoA-flavored, but it's immediately followed by re-directing attention back to the declined material rather than offering somatic grounding. Presence of signature moves with one clearly inappropriate deployment at the critical moment caps this at 2.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Curious inquiry into the hesitation — AoA-style attention to the qualifier."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Signature somatic question deployed appropriately as client is intellectualizing."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'What's it bracing against?' — near-signature defending/protection question at the right moment."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Honors the child's adaptation — consistent with AoA stance toward protective patterns."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Pattern-tracking question, generic but contextually appropriate."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "'Stay with that' — AoA-adjacent feel-it move, appropriate before the shutdown signal."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Client shows clear dissociation/freeze signals (cold, far away, flatter voice). Coach offers a gentle line but then re-directs to the feeling the client was about to feel — feel-the-unfelt-emotion deployed in an inappropriate_when context. No somatic grounding offered (feet, breath, present), no calibrate-the-threshold, no naming that this material may need a trauma-trained container."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["feeling-the-unfelt-emotion"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["calibrate-the-threshold", "the-rush-signature", "Can you feel your feet?", "What would help you slow down?", "How is your body right now? (asked early but not re-asked at shutdown)"],
  "notes": "The coach demonstrates fluency with AoA somatic and protective-pattern moves in the upper-capacity portion of the conversation, but misses the methodology's own guidance about when not to invite more feeling — specifically when dissociation signals appear. This is the classic 'signature question at the wrong moment' pattern that caps fidelity at 2."
}
